thinking.thinking: Let me start by loading the context for the ponder 'skill-command-library' and understanding what it's about.

input.command: sdlc ponder session read skill-command-library 1 && echo "---SESSION 2---" && sdlc ponder session read skill-command-library 2
sdlc milestone list && echo "---FEATURES---" && sdlc feature list

thinking.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The ponder is about a Commands and Skill Discoverability Library — specifically building a browseable `/docs/commands` page in the web UI with all 34 `sdlc-*` commands categorized.

The session 2 made a clear decision: this is a single, clean feature called `commands-docs-page`. Let me check if there's an existing milestone where this fits:

- `install-onboarding-polish` — "Installation and Onboarding Polish" — could be a fit since discoverability is part of onboarding
- `v14-new-user-experience` — "New User Experience" — this seems like a strong fit! Command discoverability is critical for new users.


- `artifact-viewer-v1` and other features don't really apply here

Looking at the feature list for `v14-new-user-experience`, it already has several onboarding-focused items like init-error-filenames, readme-install-guide, and ponder-first-onboarding, so `commands-docs-page` would fit naturally alongside those. This is a single, focused feature to implement the `/docs/commands` page with proper categorization.

The decision comes down to whether this belongs in `v14-new-user-experience` or `install-onboarding-polish`. Since command discoverability directly supports new users understanding what's available, I'll add it to `v14-new-user-experience` where it aligns with the existing onboarding and UX improvements.

text: Now I have full context. This ponder produced a clear, well-scoped single feature: a browseable commands catalog page. The spec is complete. Let me check `v14-new-user-experience` features to confirm fit:

input.command: sdlc milestone info v14-new-user-experience 2>/dev/null || echo "not found"

thinking.thinking:
The `v14-new-user-experience` milestone is about new user experience and has 5 features. Command discoverability for new users fits perfectly here. 

The ponder is a single feature scope: `commands-docs-page`. I'll create that feature and add it to `v14-new-user-experience`.

Let me do this now.

text: This fits cleanly. The commands catalog is a new-user discoverability feature — exactly what `v14-new-user-experience` is about. Single feature, add to existing milestone.

input.command: sdlc feature create commands-docs-page --title "Commands Catalog — browseable /docs/commands page with all 34 sdlc-* commands categorized"

input.description: Link ponder to the milestone it committed to

text:
Done. Here's the summary:

---

## What was created

**1 feature** added to an existing milestone:

| Feature | Milestone | What it builds |
|---|---|---|
| `commands-docs-page` | `v14-new-user-experience` | Browseable `/docs/commands` page — all 34 `sdlc-*` commands, 8 categories, copy-on-click cards, `GET /api/commands` backend |

**Scope is small and clean:** static `CommandMeta` array in sdlc-server (~100 lines), one route, one frontend component with tab-based category filter wired into DocsPage. No state machine changes. Also fixes 5 missing `gemini_description` fields as part of the work.

**Why `v14-new-user-experience`:** The core user problem (Xist couldn't figure out *when* to use each command) is a new-user discoverability problem — exactly what v14 is addressing.

---

**Next:**
```
/sdlc-run commands-docs-page
```
